capability_objective: Plan and execute independent lookups in parallel, gate dependent retrievals on uniquely resolved stable IDs, respect read-only negative constraints, and synthesize an ordered status comparison from full records.
prompt: The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local support-desk executable for all case access; do not inspect or query its seed data or runtime directory directly. The executable is `python3 support_desk.py`; its built-in help describes the available commands. Find the case named “Damaged Cold-Chain Parcel” at “Alder Foods” and the case named “Duplicate Annual Charge” at “Beacon Arts”. Your first case-data shell execution must start both independent name-and-location searches concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. After both results return, retrieve a branch only when its search produced exactly one stable ID, using only that returned ID. When both branches resolve, your next case-data shell execution must start both independent full-record retrievals concurrently from that one shell execution and wait for both. Compare the status values from the full records, not from search summaries. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Write the completed handoff to `shift-handoff.md`. When both branches resolve, that file must contain exactly three nonblank lines and no heading or other prose. List the records by ascending full-record date, formatting each of the first two lines exactly as `- <date> — <name> at <location> (<stable-id>): <status>.` The third line must be exactly `Statuses match: <status>.` when equal or `Statuses differ: <earlier-status> versus <later-status>.` when unequal. Preserve every substituted value exactly as returned by the full-record retrievals.
